Plaintiff Charles Schwab'S Motion For Sanctions Regarding Spoliation Of Evidence And Discovery (Continued From 7/26/07)
Off Calendar Jul-27-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Jeffrey B. Kirschenbaum, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Charles Schwab & Co., Inc.
Steven W. Strain, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Dennis Chen

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modification: Plaintiff Charles Schwab and Company's motion for sanctions is granted in part. The motion for evidentiary sanctions is granted. The Court finds that defendants misused the discovery process by making evasive responses. Unless and until defendants produce the home computer that was used before December 2006 and the silver thumb drive that was misplaced in the fall of 2006, defendants are prohibited from offering any testimony or other evidence relating to the use and / or contents of the computer and portable drive. The request for monetary sanctions is denied.
Parties to submit proposed order.

MODIFICATION: That order made without prejudice to seeking issued sanctions at time of trial.
COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Regarding Order Compelling Further Discovery Responses
Off Calendar Mar-27-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jeffrey B. Kirshenbaum, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Charles Schwab & Co., Inc.
Steven W. Strain, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Dennis Chen, Daniel Wu and GPS Capital Management, LLC
Garrett Sanderson, Esq., for non-parties: Fidelity Brokerage Services, LLC and National Fianacial Services.
Court Reporter: Cheryl Wilder CSR#7805

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modification: Plaintiff Charles Schwabs' motion for evidentiary and monetary sanctions is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion for evidentiary and contempt sanctions is denied. The motion for monetary sanctions is granted. The Court finds that defendant failed to comply with the prior order by providing untimely and incomplete responses. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $3,160 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Defendant is ordered to comply with the prior order.

MODIFICATION: Denial of evidentiary santions without prejudice. Defendant stipulated to produce PMK and respond to additional inerrogatories (up to 35) within 10 days. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Deposition Subpoenas ; Memo Of P And A ; Pos; Declaration
Off Calendar Mar-27-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jeffrey B. Kirshenbaum, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Charles Schwab & Co., Inc.
Steven W. Strain, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Dennis Chen, Daniel Wu and GPS Capital Management, LLC
Garrett Sanderson, Esq., for non-parties: Fidelity Brokerage Services, LLC and National Fianacial Services.
Court Reporter: Cheryl Wilder CSR#7805

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with Modification: Plaintiff Charles Schwabs' motion to enforce deposition subpoenas to TD Ameritrade Inc., Fidelity Investments Institutional Operations Company Inc., and Fidelity Investments Institutional Services Company.
The motion is granted as to TD Ameritrade Inc. as no opposition has been filed. As to the Fidelity entities, the motion is granted as to the requests that seek automated customer account transfer forms, new client application forms, and documents sufficient to establish revenues generated to defendants in connection with specified clients.

As to the request for documents sufficient to establish revenues generated to Fidelity. The parties shall be prepared to address redacting the actual revenues while producing documents that indicate individual account activity.

MODIFICATION: of producing monthly account statements. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
